description: This grayscale map displays the mean intensity of a Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) image captured with VV (Vertical transmit, Vertical receive) polarization. The X-axis represents Azimuth [pixel], ranging from 0 to approximately 1600, and the Y-axis represents Range [pixel], ranging from 0 to approximately 3000. The image shows a landscape where dark areas, particularly prominent in the upper-left, left, and lower-left, represent features with low radar backscatter, likely water bodies or very smooth surfaces. The central and lower-right areas are dominated by various shades of grey, indicating agricultural fields and other land cover types. A distinct, bright, high-intensity area is visible in the upper-right quadrant, characteristic of a densely built-up urban environment with strong radar signal backscatter. Linear features, such as roads or canals, are also discernible across the scene.
